hype.text: England doing what England does best and boring us all into a £20 refund — the only scenario where Ethan actually breaks even without selling a kidney to keep that motor on the road. Watching Argentina is basically monitoring Ethan's credit score live: one Messi masterclass and he might afford this month's PCP payment; one flop and the bailiffs get a new pin on the map. Come on Argentina, the whole group needs this pot paid out before Ethan's finance company sends a recovery truck as his celebration parade.

Bell's only chance of a return requires France to win and England to lose, a scenario that terrifies him because it means leaving the house to celebrate — his winter hermit phase was supposed to be permanent, not a preseason. The lifeguard who treats a whistle like a personality trait has drawn a team he doesn't watch, understands, or like, making him the only man in Britain praying for a French clean sheet while Googling 'offside rule for dummies.' If England scrape through, the pot refunds and the whole group gets their twenty quid back, the ultimate anticlimax that renders Bell's accidental ownership the most expensive insect-hunting trip in history.

Will's got Spain, which is handy because his PhD thesis on "structural integrity of playground equipment" is the only thing keeping that defence upright after he threw his back out saving toddlers in Prague. Ethan's Argentina side mirrors the man perfectly: all flashy attack, zero engine, and financed on a balloon payment he'll be explaining to the bailiffs until 2035. One's carrying a nation on a knackered spine, the other's carrying negative equity on a German motor — perfect sweepstake material.
